data.heroTitle: Building the infrastructure before the infrastructure.
data.heroBody: Most of this month went into the scaffolding around your projects, not the projects themselves — rotation key backup strategies for Tranquil PDS, licensing policy reviews for Recap Time Squad, elective-track sequencing for 2Y1, pricing math across your whole AI tooling stack. You used Claude as a researcher and a second pair of eyes on technical decisions, handing off the fact-gathering and then steering the conclusions yourself. The pattern across all of it: you'd lay out your setup, ask for the gap you hadn't spotted, and then decide what to do with it on your own time.

topics.title: AT Proto infrastructure
topics.description: PDS deployment on Uberspace, disaster recovery drills, rotation key backup, did:plc for services

topics.title: STI College academics
topics.description: Grade review, elective track sequencing, TESDA AI course activities and curriculum ideas

topics.title: AI tooling and pricing
topics.description: Claude Pro vs Google AI Plus pricing, free-tier limits, Zed Student plan, Copilot budget

topics.title: Open-source governance
topics.description: Recap Time Squad licensing review, AT Proto trademark discourse, fediverse protocol politics

topics.title: Personal user manual
topics.description: Integrating preferences into AI custom instructions, MCP server vs raw Markdown, memory updates

aboutYourTime.title: Keep pressure-testing the PDS setup before it goes live.
aboutYourTime.body: Four separate conversations touched Tranquil PDS — Postgres backup strategy, rotation key redundancy, the 72-hour recovery window, and the Margin.at lost-key case study. Each one added a new failure scenario to the DR runbook.

aboutYourTime.title: The 2Y1 elective decision is still open.
aboutYourTime.body: You caught that IT Elective 1 locks in the entire prerequisite chain, and that it lands this term — not Year 4 like you'd been told. The RAF will confirm which course is filling that slot.

aboutYourTime.title: Hold the line on what the AI stack actually costs you.
aboutYourTime.body: You mapped every tool — Copilot at $2/month capped, Zed Student at zero, Google AI Pro on a free trial until October — and named the FOMO for what it was before spending anything new.

worthThinkingAbout.title: You research decisions longer than you spend making them.
worthThinkingAbout.body: The PDS deployment, the elective track, the Claude Pro question, the Google AI tier — each got multiple rounds of fact-gathering with Claude, and each ended with you saying you'd decide later on your own. Seven of fourteen conversations this month closed that way.

expandingYourSkills.title: Research goes to Claude, but every call stays with you.
expandingYourSkills.body: The PDS backup architecture, the elective prerequisite chain, the AI subscription math — you'd hand off the lookup and then close with "I'll take care of it myself" or "will report back once I have the RAF."

expandingYourSkills.title: Your setup context arrives before the question does.
expandingYourSkills.body: The Uberspace thread opened with your GitLab CI pipeline, your 1Password key storage, your R2 pricing calculator screenshot, and your Cloudflare account topology — all before you asked anything. The elective conversation came with the 2024 program guide PDF attached.
expandingYourSkills.prompt: You are helping me plan disaster recovery for a self-hosted AT Proto PDS on Uberspace 8 Beta. Here is my current setup: Tranquil PDS compiled from source via GitLab CI, Postgres on the Asteroid's local instance, rotation keys in 1Password and dotenvx-encrypted in the repo, blobs on Cloudflare R2 as primary storage. Review my backup architecture for gaps I haven't spotted.

expandingYourSkills.title: Other people's failures are your test cases.
expandingYourSkills.body: You pulled in Ewan Croft's botched-migration postmortem and Scan's lost Margin.at rotation key thread as real-world evidence, then used both to add specific drill scenarios to your own runbook rather than taking them at face value.

expandingYourSkills.title: What goes into memory gets reviewed before it sticks.
expandingYourSkills.body: You walked through every line of your saved memory blocks, caught the overlap between Gemini and Claude custom instructions, and corrected the homelab setup, student status, location, and code-style entries one by one rather than letting stale facts persist.
